description:
AI-Powered Draft Reply Generation for Smart Label Emails

Extend the existing Smart Labels classification pipeline to auto-generate AI-drafted email replies at the moment of classification, surfaced inline within the unified inbox for frictionless review and send. Each draft is calibrated by a rolling, token-budgeted per-label context window that captures recent communication patterns — including sent replies — so Claude can match established tone, vocabulary, and intent. The context window is further enriched with Kanban card data associated with the same label, giving Claude awareness of active work items when composing replies.

---

Requirements:

TOGGLE & CONFIGURATION

- Add a per-label "Auto-Draft Reply" toggle within the Smart Labels settings UI
- Toggle state is persisted to the database per label record
- Toggle must be independently configurable per label (e.g., auto-draft enabled for "Invoices" but disabled for "Newsletters")
- Toggle is off by default

---

PER-LABEL CONTEXT WINDOW

Schema & Storage
- Create a dedicated `smart_label_context_entries` table with foreign keys linking each entry to both its parent label and its originating email
- Each entry stores: sender, subject, date received, truncated body excerpt (max ~300 tokens), and — where applicable — a truncated excerpt of the user's sent reply (this is the highest-signal field for tone calibration)
- Store whether the user sent a reply and, if so, the reply excerpt; entries without a reply excerpt are still valuable but are lower-priority context signals

Eviction & Maintenance
- When a new email is classified under a label, append it to that label's context window and evict the oldest entry once the window exceeds the configured maximum (default: 7 entries; configurable per label up to 10)
- Context window population is non-blocking and must not delay inbox display or classification completion

Token Budget Enforcement
- Before injecting the context window into any Claude prompt, calculate an estimated token count for the combined entries
- If the total exceeds a hard cap of 1,500 tokens for the entire context block, truncate body excerpts proportionally across entries — never truncate reply excerpts, as they are the highest-signal data
- The 1,500-token cap preserves headroom for the system prompt, current email body, and reply generation output

Search Index Exclusion
- Context window entries must be excluded from all full-text and semantic (pgvector) search indexes to prevent polluting search results with internal pipeline artifacts
- Context window entries must never appear in any user-facing UI — they are injected silently into the draft generation prompt only

Kanban Card Integration
- When building the context window for a given label, query the Kanban board for all active (non-archived) cards that share the same Smart Label tag or were created via that label's extraction pipeline
- Include a compact Kanban block in the context window containing: card title, current status column, priority level, due date (if set), and a one-sentence description excerpt
- Apply a separate token budget for the Kanban block (suggested: 400 tokens maximum), prioritizing cards that are overdue or in active columns (e.g., "In Progress") over backlog or completed cards
- Kanban card data in the context block is read-only context for prompt enrichment — it does not create, update, or move cards as a side effect of draft generation
- This gives Claude awareness of active work items when composing replies (e.g., referencing a known delivery date from an open card when drafting a vendor response)

---

DRAFT GENERATION PIPELINE

Trigger
- When an incoming email is classified under a label with the toggle enabled, trigger draft generation immediately after classification completes
- Draft generation runs asynchronously — classification and inbox display complete first; the draft indicator appears once generation finishes

Prompt Construction (Two-Layer Structure)
- Layer 1 — Label context block: inject the per-label context window (emails + Kanban cards, token-budgeted as above), framed as "Recent emails in this category, how they were handled, and related active work items" — signal patterns without instructing Claude to mimic them verbatim
- Layer 2 — Current email block: pass the full email body, subject, sender, and any extracted metadata (action items, key points) as the primary task

- Prompt Claude to generate a contextually appropriate, professional reply draft that is tonally consistent with how similar emails under this label have been handled

Storage
- Store draft content in a dedicated `email_drafts` table with a foreign key to the originating email
- Track draft status with at minimum: `pending_review`, `sent`, `discarded`

Error Handling
- If draft generation fails (AI error, timeout, rate limit), log the failure silently and do not show a draft indicator on the inbox row — do not surface AI errors inline

---

INBOX UI — DRAFT INDICATOR & PREVIEW

- Display a distinct "Draft Ready" icon (pencil or document-with-pen from Lucide React) on any inbox row with an associated `pending_review` draft
- Render a collapsible draft preview strip below the incoming message preview in the inbox list, showing the first 1–2 lines of the draft body and a "Review Draft" CTA
- The preview strip must be visually distinct from the incoming message: use a muted background, dashed left border, and italic text to prevent confusion between received and unsent content
- Clicking "Review Draft" opens a full draft editor pre-populated with the AI-generated content, using the existing `@uiw/react-md-editor` or a plain textarea, allowing the user to edit before sending
- Draft previews must be excluded from full-text and semantic search indexes

---

SENDING & LIFECYCLE

Send
- "Send" dispatches the reply via the appropriate provider: Microsoft Graph API for Exchange accounts, Gmail API for Gmail accounts, using the existing email integration layer
- On successful send: update draft status to `sent`, remove the preview strip from the inbox row, and append a context window entry for this email that includes both the incoming message excerpt and the sent reply excerpt — this is the highest-value signal for future draft calibration

Discard
- "Discard" sets draft status to `discarded` and removes the preview strip from the inbox row
- Discarded drafts are soft-deleted with a configurable grace period (default: 7 days) to allow recovery
- Discarded drafts do not update the label context window — the user's rejection signals the reply was not representative of their communication pattern

Regenerate
- "Regenerate" is available from within the editor and after a discard
- Triggers a new AI call using the same two-layer prompt structure with the current context window state at the time of regeneration — not the state at the time of initial classification

---

EDGE CASES & CONSTRAINTS

- Only generate a draft if the incoming email has a replyable sender address; exclude no-reply addresses, bounce addresses, and mailing list senders detected via standard headers (List-Unsubscribe, Precedence: bulk/list, etc.)
- If an email matches multiple labels and more than one has the toggle enabled, generate a single draft using the highest-priority matching label as the prompt context and context window source — do not generate duplicate drafts
- Draft generation is always non-blocking; the inbox list and classification pipeline must never wait on AI generation to complete

---


Maybe we need to add a memory feature to Smart Labels such that the user could see and edit the memory associated with the Smart Label.

summary:
## Summary: AI-Powered Draft Reply Generation for Smart Label Emails

### Changes Implemented

**Database Schema**
- Added `auto_draft_enabled` (boolean, default false) and `context_window_max` (integer, default 7) columns to `smart_labels` table
- Created `smart_label_context_entries` table for per-label memory/context window with RLS policies
- Created `email_draft_status` enum (`pending_review`, `sent`, `discarded`)
- Created `email_drafts` table for AI-generated reply drafts with RLS policies
- Created migration `0033_smart-label-drafts.sql` with idempotent DDL

**Per-Label Context Window**
- `contextWindow.ts`: Full context window management — add/evict/delete entries, token-budgeted prompt block builder
- Enforces 1,500 token cap for context block, 400 token cap for Kanban block
- Never truncates reply excerpts (highest-signal data); proportionally truncates body excerpts when over budget
- Kanban card integration: queries active cards matching label name/tag, prioritizes overdue/in-progress cards

**Draft Generation Pipeline**
- `draftGeneration.ts`: Two-layer prompt structure (label context + current email) with AI reply generation
- No-reply sender detection (noreply, mailer-daemon, bounce patterns) and bulk mail header filtering
- Non-blocking trigger after classification — fires and forgets, never blocks inbox/classification
- Duplicate draft prevention (checks for existing pending_review draft)
- Context window auto-population on classification

**Smart Labels Admin UI**
- Per-label "Auto-Draft Reply" toggle switch with immediate persistence
- Context window size selector (3/5/7/10 entries) shown when auto-draft is enabled
- "Auto-Draft" badge in label card header when enabled
- "View Memory" button that expands an inline memory viewer showing all context entries
- Memory entries display sender, subject, date, body excerpt, reply status, and reply excerpt
- Individual entry deletion from the memory viewer

**Inbox Draft Indicator & Preview**
- Blue pencil icon in status indicators column for emails with pending drafts
- "Draft Ready" badge next to smart label chips in email row
- Collapsible draft preview strip below each email row with dashed blue left border and muted background
- Preview shows first ~120 chars of draft body with "Review Draft" CTA
- Expanded editor with textarea pre-populated with AI-generated draft

**Send/Discard/Regenerate Lifecycle**
- Send: marks draft as `sent`, updates context window with reply excerpt for future tone calibration
- Discard: marks as `discarded`, removes preview strip (soft-delete, does not update context window)
- Regenerate: discards current draft, generates new draft using current context window state
- All actions update UI state immediately

**API Routes**
- `GET/DELETE /api/smart-labels/:id/context` — View and manage context entries
- `POST /api/smart-labels/drafts` — Batch-fetch pending drafts for email IDs
- `GET/PATCH /api/smart-labels/drafts/:draftId` — Get/update individual drafts (send/discard/regenerate)

**AI Prompt**
- Added `PROMPT_SMART_LABEL_DRAFT_REPLY` prompt key and default text to prompts registry
- User-customizable via existing custom prompts admin UI

### Files Modified
- `lib/db/schema.ts` — Added `autoDraftEnabled`, `contextWindowMax` to smartLabels; added `smartLabelContextEntries` and `emailDrafts` tables
- `lib/smartLabels/labels.ts` — Updated SELECT queries and `updateSmartLabel` to include new columns
- `lib/smartLabels/classify.ts` — Added draft generation trigger after classification
- `lib/ai/prompts.ts` — Added `PROMPT_SMART_LABEL_DRAFT_REPLY` key and default prompt definition
- `lib/validators/schemas.ts` — Added `autoDraftEnabled` and `contextWindowMax` to `updateSmartLabelSchema`
- `types/smartLabel.ts` — Added `SmartLabelContextEntry`, `EmailDraft` interfaces; added new fields to `SmartLabel`
- `app/(app)/admin/smart-labels/SmartLabelsClient.tsx` — Added auto-draft toggle, context window size, memory viewer UI
- `app/(app)/inbox/page.tsx` — Added draft state, draft fetching, draft indicator, preview strip with send/discard/regenerate

### Files Created
- `lib/smartLabels/contextWindow.ts` — Context window management and token-budgeted prompt building
- `lib/smartLabels/draftGeneration.ts` — Draft generation pipeline with no-reply detection
- `app/api/smart-labels/[id]/context/route.ts` — Context entries API
- `app/api/smart-labels/drafts/route.ts` — Batch draft lookup API
- `app/api/smart-labels/drafts/[draftId]/route.ts` — Individual draft management API
- `drizzle/migrations/0033_smart-label-drafts.sql` — Database migration

### Notes for Developer
- Migration `0033` must be applied to the database before using this feature
- The `auto_draft_enabled` toggle is **off by default** for all labels
- Actual email sending via Graph API/Gmail API is not implemented yet — the "Send" action marks the draft as sent and updates the context window, but does not dispatch the email through the provider. This is intentional since the codebase has no outbound email infrastructure yet
- Fixed a pre-existing pattern issue: gmail_emails uses UUID primary keys, so `parseInt()` was removed for gmail queries in the new draft generation code
- TypeScript compilation passes cleanly with zero errors
